title: Add --all to podman start
body:
closes #8779 

### Progress

- [x] Add `--all` flag to restart all containers in `podman start`
- [x] ~Add to `--filter` to `podman start`. This flag allows to filter for specific attributes of the containers. For instance, a filter to match a specific restart policy.~ moved to #9689
- [x] Add tests to the changes above
- [x] Update `podman start` documents accordingly
- [ ] Ship a systemd service (similar to podman-auto-update.service) that package managers would install on the system.
- [x] ~podman systemm boot~

comments.body: This is not going to make the 3.0 release, moving to 3.1
@boaz0 Could you rebase this please.
Just did
Ummm, I have no idea why openshift bot doesn't remove the tag, though.
@boaz0 now PRs are stopped, based on requiring a test.  Could you add one, to get by this?
Yea, that's what I am doing right now. Thanks for the support @rhatdan
Hey, friendly ping here. Any updates 😅

@UebelAndre 
The code is ready to be reviewed. What is left for me to do is to update the docs and work on the service file that will be added to the podman package.
@TomSweeneyRedHat are the failing tests related to my change?

@boaz0 it sure looks like it. What I'm seeing is:
```console
$ podman run -d first
cid1
$ podman run -d --restart=always second
cid2
$ podman start --all
cid2           <--- whereas you seemed to be expecting cid1 and cid2
```
Can you confirm that your `start --all` code is working as you expect?

Also, I'm going to be offering some review comments in a few moments.
@boaz0 I think @edsantiago has answered the test questions.
@edsantiago your comments were so helpful!!! Thank you so much :bow:

I still don't understand why this is happening:
```
hack/xref-helpmsgs-manpages
xref-helpmsgs-manpages: podman container start --help lists --all, but --all not in docs/source/markdown/podman-start.1.md
xref-helpmsgs-manpages: podman start --help lists --all, but --all not in docs/source/markdown/podman-start.1.md
```

"--all" does appear in podman-start.1.md.
@edsantiago I'll point to you on this one - it looks like it ought to work? Any idea what need to be added/removed/reordered to make the checker happy?

LGTM but tests are racey (see comments). The race seems very unlikely to trigger, but I'd really prefer race-free tests. 

Unfortunately, I can't think of a way. This has been on my mind for the past week, and every day I come back to revisit it, and every day decide I have no new ideas for fixing it.
@boaz0 the test failures look real: somehow podman-remote is trying to restart the running container.
@edsantiago - that's why it failed :fearful:. I will add it to podman-remote. Thanks.
@boaz0 exactly the same failure again

Can you be more specific about what you ran, and what you mean by "it worked"?

On my Fedora 33 system, I can 100% reproduce the CI failure via:
```console
window1:
$ sudo bin/podman system service --timeout=0
```
```console
window2:
$ sudo env PODMAN=$(pwd)/bin/podman-remote bats test/system/045-start.bats
```
With `podman` instead of `podman-remote`, the test passes.
Odd - looking at the code they all seem the same. I am investigating this further.

OK I think I found the error. @edsantiago can you confirm this:
https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/8828/files#diff-7458c1a055a350fe316f106abaf9936dd993da3e524faf64bad09f3a3ae01a68R606-R607
These two lines were supposed to be inside the `if !ctrRunning { ... }`. And because of that I will see containers that are not running in the output.
@boaz0 I don't grok that area of code, sorry. @mheon or @rhatdan WDYT?

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *boaz0*, *edsantiago*, *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [edsantiago,rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Looks like you figured it out! (As in, "got tests passing" :-). LGTM but I would like close scrutiny of the new change from @containers/podman-maintainers 

You've put a lot of energy into this one. Thank you for your perseverance and patience.

/lgtm
@boaz0 Thanks.
What a legend! Thank you @boaz0!!!

@UebelAndre I didn't do much. I am standing on the shoulders of giants @edsantiago and @rhatdan - they guided me and helped a lot. :bow: :pray: :clap: 

Good job team!
